perturbation.original_text: (v) In the event that UBS AG provides the information referred to above, such information shall be provided not later than five (5) business days prior to the date in which the Master Servicer is required to file a Form 10-D for such Distribution Date.
perturbation.changed_text: (v) In the event that UBS AG provides the information referred to above, such information shall be provided not later than one (1) business day prior to the date in which the Master Servicer is required to file a Form 10-D for such Distribution Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a five-business-day window for providing information, which allows for reasonable preparation time. Reducing this to one business day creates an unreasonable and potentially unachievable timeframe, potentially violating SEC regulations regarding timely and accurate financial reporting.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text imposes a one-day deadline, increasing the risk of non-compliance with the SEC's requirements for timely and accurate filing of Form 10-D. This filing necessitates the provision of pertinent data from the counterparty of derivative contracts, and insufficient time could lead to errors or delays, breaching SEC regulations.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The change from five business days to one business day for providing information related to Form 10-D filing introduces a significant risk of non-compliance with SEC regulations, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 17 CFR 240.13a-13, pertaining to quarterly reports on Form 10-Q, suggesting the importance of timely financial reporting. While it doesn't directly address the specific timeframe for information provision, it implies a regulatory expectation for accurate and timely filings. The reduced timeframe increases the potential for errors or delays, contradicting the implied need for adequate preparation time to ensure compliance with SEC regulations, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Rating Agency Downgrade: If a Ratings Event occurs with respect to UBS AG, then UBS AG shall, at its own expense, (i) assign this Transaction hereunder to a third party within thirty (30) days of such Ratings Event that meets or exceeds, or as to which any applicable credit support provider meets or exceeds, the Approved Rating Threshold (as defined below) on terms substantially similar to this Confirmation or (ii) deliver collateral acceptable in a form and amount acceptable to Fitch Ratings ("Fitch") and Moody's Investors Service Inc. ("Moody's) within thirty (30) days of such Ratings Event and subject to written confirmation from Fitch and Moody's that delivery of such collateral in the context of such downgrade will not result in a withdrawal, qualification or downgrade of the then current ratings assigned to the Certificates.
perturbation.changed_text: Rating Agency Downgrade: If a Ratings Event occurs with respect to UBS AG, then UBS AG shall, at its own expense, (i) assign this Transaction hereunder to a third party within ten (10) days of such Ratings Event that meets or exceeds, or as to which any applicable credit support provider meets or exceeds, the Approved Rating Threshold (as defined below) on terms substantially similar to this Confirmation or (ii) deliver collateral acceptable in a form and amount acceptable to Fitch Ratings ("Fitch") and Moody's Investors Service Inc. ("Moody's) within ten (10) days of such Ratings Event and subject to written confirmation from Fitch and Moody's that delivery of such collateral in the context of such downgrade will not result in a withdrawal, qualification or downgrade of the then current ratings assigned to the Certificates.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the timeframe for UBS AG to assign the transaction or deliver collateral from 30 days to 10 days may violate regulations regarding adequate time for financial institutions to rectify ratings deficiencies and ensure the stability of mortgage-backed securities.
perturbation.law_explanation: 12 CFR § 390.76 establishes standards for asset-backed securities. Shortening the timeframe to 10 days could lead to non-compliance. The regulations require appropriate risk management procedures, which involve giving institutions enough time to address ratings downgrades to protect the underlying assets of mortgage-backed securities and, ultimately, investors.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The change from 30 days to 10 days for UBS AG to address a Ratings Event directly contradicts the requirement for adequate time to rectify ratings deficiencies, as described in the Perturbation and Law explanations. The Scraped Law Snippet 1 indicates limited access and the existence of regulatory guidelines, reinforcing the importance of compliance with official standards. Shortening the timeframe could lead to non-compliance, violating the risk management procedures needed to protect the underlying assets, as suggested by the Law Explanation.
